Title: A multicenter, randomized phase 2 study to establish combinations of CBP501, cisplatin and nivolumab for ≥3rd-line treatment of patients with advanced pancreatic adenocarcinoma
Authors: T Enzler, A Nguyen, J Misleh, V J Cline, M Johns, N Shumway, S Paulson, R Siegel, T Larson, W Messersmith, D Richards, J Chaves, E Pierce, M Zalupski, V Sahai, D Orr, S A Ruste, A Haun, T Kawabe
Filename: A multicenter, randomized phase 2 study to establish combinations of CBP501.pdf
Treatment Regimen:
- Names of all drugs/agents in the combination: CBP501, cisplatin, nivolumab
- Doses and administration schedule for each agent: CBP501 (16 or 25 mg/m^2), cisplatin (60 mg/m^2), nivolumab (240 mg), administered every 3 weeks
- Route of administration: Intravenous (IV)
- Cycle length and duration: 21-day cycle, with a maximum of four cycles for combination therapy
- Whether tested as monotherapy vs. combination: Tested as combination therapy
- Line of therapy: ≥3rd-line treatment
- Any lead-in or sequential dosing protocols: No lead-in; sequential dosing allowed for nivolumab alone after four cycles if progression-free
Clinical Outcomes:
- Overall Response Rate (ORR): 22.2% in arm 1 (CBP(25)/CDDP/nivo), with 2 partial responses; no responses in other arms.
- Disease Control Rate (DCR): 33.3% for CBP(25)/CDDP/nivo and CDDP/nivo arms; 11.1% for CBP(16)/CDDP/nivo; 0% for CBP(25)/CDDP.
- Progression-Free Survival (PFS): Median PFS was 2.4 months for CBP(25)/CDDP/nivo, 2.1 months for CBP(16)/CDDP/nivo, 1.5 months for CBP(25)/CDDP, and 1.5 months for CDDP/nivo.
- Overall Survival (OS): Median OS was 6.3 months for CBP(25)/CDDP/nivo, 5.3 months for CBP(16)/CDDP/nivo, 3.7 months for CBP(25)/CDDP, and 4.9 months for CDDP/nivo.
- Duration of Response (DOR): Median DOR was 4.1 months for CBP(25)/CDDP/nivo.
- No biomarker-based subgroup analyses or statistical significance and confidence intervals mentioned.
Safety Profile:
- Treatment-related adverse events by grade: 88% grade 1-2, 12% grade 3, no grade 4-5.
- Most common adverse events: nausea (27%), diarrhea (12%), fatigue (42%), infusion-related reaction (58%), neutrophil count decreased (15%), decreased appetite (15%).
- Serious adverse events: one case of acute kidney injury related to CBP501 and cisplatin.
- Treatment discontinuations: one due to grade 2 infusion-related reaction.
- Dose reductions or delays: managed by limiting cycles and temporary infusion interruptions.
- Treatment-related deaths: none reported.
- Tolerability assessment: overall well tolerated with manageable toxicities.
- Differences in safety between monotherapy vs combination arms: higher incidence of infusion-related reactions in combination arms.
Study Identifiers: Not mentioned (the paper does not provide a specific trial name or acronym)

- Whether tested as monotherapy vs. combination: Both monotherapy (Acalabrutinib alone) and combination (Acalabrutinib + Pembrolizumab)

- Treatment discontinuations: 14.3% in monotherapy, 18.4% in combination therapy.
